generic_rules: Focus repairs on resolving AGREE syntax errors reported by the validator.
Prioritize repairs that address unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations.
Repairs should not introduce new architecture elements; modify existing contracts only.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts reside within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` keywords for contract elements.
Implementation-level bindings (e.g., `assign`) are valid only within implementation annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes.
Avoid using subcomponent paths or implementation-only references in component type annexes.
Do not attempt to fix AGREE errors by modifying the underlying AADL architecture.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction (component types, implementations, features, connections, properties, and owners)
Compact requirement semantics and model traceability
AGREE contract patterns for assumptions and guarantees
